brief:
GAP/RATIONALE: Evidence on left atrial appendage closure (LAAC) in patients who have LAA thrombus is scarce. Prior reported cases mostly used transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) for guidance. Intracardiac echocardiography (ICE) is now common in LAAC but its use specifically in the setting of LAA thrombus is unproven. Claimed to be the first study reporting feasibility of ICE-guided LAAC in patients with LAA thrombus.

DESIGN/SETTING: Single-arm observational feasibility study (case series). Patients underwent a modified ICE-guided LAAC procedure between June 2021 and April 2023. Periprocedural events and follow-up clinical outcomes recorded.

POPULATION/N: 12 patients total. Indication: persistent LAA thrombus despite anticoagulation, or contraindications to anticoagulation. Breakdown: 10 with persistent LAA thrombus, 2 with anticoagulation contraindications. Mean age 65 +/- 7 years; 92% male.

THROMBUS LOCATION: apex n=6; body n=3; ostium n=3.

INTERVENTION: LAAC using the LAmbre device, guided by ICE (no concurrent comparison group). Cerebral protection device used in n=11.

KEY RESULTS:
- LAmbre device successfully implanted in 100% of patients under ICE guidance.
- No thrombotic material retrieved in the 11 patients who had cerebral protection.
- No severe periprocedural complications in any patient.
- All patients completed TEE follow-up: no device-related thrombus detected; no peridevice leak >3 mm detected.
- During median follow-up 147 days (IQR 80-306 days): no stroke/TIA, no systemic embolism, no major bleeding events.

TAKEAWAY: ICE-guided LAAC with the LAmbre device may be feasible in patients with LAA thrombus when performed by experienced operators.
